超級棒的，用好幾年了，決定要來支持一下買付費版本的，拿來播FLAC超級方便！

而求功能越來越多，但是一樣的穩定，可以說是iOS上面最好用的音樂播放軟體了！

author: Sun........!
title: 軟體很好用，但是我的手機打不開會一直閃退
content: 自從之前的更新之後，我iOS 13.3的iPhone就一直打不開程式，每次都會閃退。不論是重新下載，甚至是iPhone重置後依然存在這個問題，本來想說之後的更新應該就會修正了，但這個問題似乎沒有被發現，如果製作團隊有看到，再麻煩你們修正了，謝謝！這個軟體的排版，以及中文化的介面老實說是我至目前為止遇過數一數二厲害的，而且在歌曲的聆聽與編輯上面都十分順手，所以我覺得他值得五顆星。

author: zutomayo lover
title: Can connect to pc via smb, good!
content:
I downloaded 7 music player apps that are able to play FLAC, and this the only one that is able to “stream” music files from my laptop (and the external drive connected to it). Just click on the files/folders and music plays, no need to manually copy them onto my iPad. Neat.
There is one problem however, which I believe has to do with folders that have Japanese names, specifically hiragana with dots. The tunes in those folders show up but can’t be downloaded or played. When I click “Open,” an error message showed up:
Error
code 2: Open failed with (Oxc000003a)
STATUS OBJECT PATH NOT FOUND.
Hopefully this can be fixed in the future, or I would have to change my folder names to English.

author: Bocheng Gu
title: Problematic SMB streaming/playback in public
content:
First off, this is a great app for streaming FLAC music from my PC or NAS *at home*. BUT when I try to use it outside in public places to stream from my wifi-direct card reader (i.e. Kingston MobileLite Wireless G3 or Apotop DW21), it's absolutely nightmare! Below is the problems I encountered with the app listed in order from the most severe to the least. The post is quite long because I try to be specific with the issues so that they may be easier for you to identify and hopefully fix in future updates. So bear with me.

1. The SMB connection only works in purely wifi environment. Whenever and wherever my device is connected to BOTH the wifi from my wireless card reader AND the 4G/3G mobile network, the app just wouldn't let me access my wireless card reader at all! Yet there's no such problem when I use other SMB streaming apps (like nPlayer) to stream from the same source. So I'm pretty sure it's the problem with this particular app, which brings me to my next issue.

2. The app can access the files on my wireless card reader ONLY when I cut off the mobile network and let my device connect to only my wifi card reader without the internet access (Specifically mobile network. Wifi bridge works fine though). With the above settings, the app works well at streaming FLAC from my wireless card reader *at home*. The playback is smooth, no glitches or pauses between songs whatsoever. HOWEVER, when I try to use it outside in public places with the same devices and same setup, everything changes! The app still can stream my FLAC files but it always takes about 30 seconds to load the next FLAC file, yet if the next song is in MP3 or AAC format it plays immediately. For an app that specializes in streaming FLAC music, this is totally unacceptable! And I know this is an inherent issue with the app because *yet again* I encountered no such playback problem with other SMB streaming apps. nPlayer for example streams my FLAC files just fine everywhere (at home or outside) and with or without connecting to mobile network. This app however only seems to work smoothly in SMB mode at home in purely wifi environment, which renders it pretty much pointless since streaming FLAC music outside is my main goal for this app.

3. This is pretty minor compared to the first two main issues. But  for some reason the app wouldn't display the album art for some songs, which brings me to my next gripe.

4. I much prefer the old UI where during playback you see just the album art without the pause button in the center and no title bar in the bottom, both of which IMHO are completely redundant since the app already shows the title of the song on top and the control buttons at the bottom. Also the rotation animation of the album art is a bit annoying. Can we get rid of that too? Or at least have the ability to turn it off?

Finally, thank you for your patience and time. I'm looking forward to your reply and possible solutions for the issues I listed above.

My Setup:

Streaming devices: iPhone SE or iPad mini 4, both have been updated to the latest iOS version 10.3.1

Streaming sources: Apotop Wi-Copy (DW21) or Kingston MobileLite Wireless G3 (MLWG3), both use 2.4g wifi-direct to connect to my iPhone or iPad.
